feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Column | Six things I did to rid my yard of mosquitoes when nothing else worked
entry.summary: This neighborhood plan mimics ones used to eradicate the insects on islands. Could it work for my house?

entry.title: ADHD is linked to chronic pain and other conditions. Here’s what we know.
entry.summary: A growing body of evidence suggests that people with ADHD may be at risk for anxiety, disordered eating, migraines, long covid and other problems.

entry.title: The Pacemaker Patch
entry.summary: A pacemaker is implanted to send signals that regulate a patient’s heartbeat, and to do that, you need power. That means they require battery changes, and when the device in question happens …

entry.title: What to know about alpha-gal syndrome, the life-threatening meat allergy caused by tick bites
entry.summary: Health officials are raising awareness about alpha-gal syndrome, a meat allergy triggered by tick bites. This condition is on the rise. Symptoms include hives, diarrhea and itchiness after eating meat or dairy. The allergy doesn’t impact consumption of seafoo…

entry.title: The fight over America's vaccine future
entry.summary: A pivotal series of decisions in the next few months will determine the shape of U.S. vaccine policy for years to come.

entry.title: Dogs and humans are more alike than we thought, study finds
entry.summary: The same biological signals that help predict lifespan in humans also appear in dogs, according to new research from the Dog Aging Project—a finding that could help scientists better understand aging in both species.

entry.title: Anaphylaxis concerns for Cardiff boy, 10, with 20 allergies
entry.summary: Yann Jennings, 10, has allergies including seafood, cats and coconut, but can now eat some nuts daily.

entry.title: 445 birds euthanized due to bird flu outbreak at Rhode Island market
entry.summary: The Rhode Island Department of Health and Rhode Island Department of Environmental Management said that 445 birds at a bird market in Providence were euthanized

entry.title: Older adults who took a weekly fifteen-minute walk for eight weeks felt measurably better, but the difference came down to one instruction: those told to seek out small moments of awe reported more gratitude and compassion and less daily distress
entry.summary: “Experiencing awe is such a simple practice, just taking a moment to look out the window or pausing to consider the technological marvels that surround us, and we now show it can have measurable effects on our emotional well-being,” said Virginia Sturm, an as…

entry.title: Vitamin C levels in blood plasma linked with brain connectivity and volume in older adults
entry.summary: A study of 2,044 older Japanese adults found that those with lower vitamin C levels in their blood plasma tended to have a lower volume of gray matter in their brains, as well as lower connectivity among a collection of brain regions known as the default mode…

entry.title: Deadly dog virus is spreading across Arizona. Veterinarians say it's preventable.
entry.summary: A deadly dog virus is spreading rapidly across Arizona, with cases up 250% compared to the same time last year, and an emergency veterinarian is warning...

entry.title: Afternoon Habits That Can Quietly Work Against Heart Health
entry.summary: Find out which afternoon choices cardiologists say can have a lasting impact on heart health.

entry.title: Global HIV prevention declined drastically after Trump aid cuts, U.N. finds
entry.summary: Cuts from the U.S. and others have hit hard, with the number of people receiving PrEP dropping by 38% from 2024 to 2025, according to data from 62 countries.

entry.title: Sugar-free, low-fat diet tied to insulin resistance in mouse study
entry.summary: Mice kept on a sugar-free, low-fat diet developed insulin resistance and other metabolic changes in a recent study.

entry.title: Millennials Like Me Were Sold a Fantasy About Weed. Now Researchers Have a Warning.
entry.summary: This wasn’t at all what I imagined when I first lit up.

entry.title: Ozempic Users Are Skimping Out on Exercise, Study Finds
entry.summary: People's daily steps might drop after starting a GLP-1 prescription, new research suggests.

entry.title: Millions Take This Joint Supplement but Scientists Found a Concerning Alzheimer’s Link
entry.summary: Scientists found a concerning Alzheimer’s link involving glucosamine, a joint supplement used by millions.

entry.title: Brushing Your Teeth Could Help Prevent a Deadly Hospital Infection
entry.summary: You go to hospital for treatment and to get better.

entry.title: A New Path to Preventing Cancer
entry.summary: A Landmark Study Showing the Way Forward

entry.title: Golden Retriever Says Yes To Seeing His ‘Babies,’ Then Rushes Over To Snuggle Them
entry.summary: The interaction between this adorable Golden Retriever dog named Boojeaux and his “babies,” little chicks, is absolutely heartwarming, and it will brighten u...

entry.title: Not every cancer begins with pain: Doctors reveal the everyday symptoms people often ignore for too long
entry.summary: Most people imagine cancer begins with severe pain, a large lump, or a dramatic health crisis. The reality is often much quieter.A cough that refuses to go away. Constant tiredness that gets blamed on work stress. Bloating that feels like a digestive issue. A…

entry.title: How the Best Drinks for Gut Health Can Improve Your Digestion (and Overall Health)
entry.summary: These are some of the best ways to get pro- and prebiotics into your diet.

entry.title: Divorcees and widowed share concerning mental health trait, researchers find
entry.summary: New research from Norway finds divorcees and the widowed may face higher mortality risk than those who remain coupled, highlighting social disconnection.

entry.title: Harmonic radar tags reveal how mosquitoes move through fields and parkland
entry.summary: It's an insect everybody loves to hate. Pesky mosquitoes will be out in swarms as the weather warms up across the U.S.—and their bites aren't just itchy. They can transmit pathogens that can cause diseases like West Nile virus, Zika virus and malaria, to name…

entry.title: It’s not Ebola you need to be worried about with the World Cup. These are the real health risks.
entry.summary: That dip sitting out for hours in the heat poses a bigger health risk than Ebola, according to physicians preparing for the World Cup in New Jersey.

entry.title: The #1 Nutrient Men over 40 Should Pay More Attention to, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Protein is the top nutrient for men over 40 to support muscle, metabolism, and healthy aging, experts say. Learn easy ways to get more protein daily.

entry.title: Doctors Inject Human Subjects With First Vaccine Designed by AI
entry.summary: An AI-designed vaccine has already completed its first human trial and could provide universal protection against entire families of viruses.

entry.title: A mom's Stage IV cancer had no symptoms. An innovative surgery saved her life.
entry.summary: A chance emergency room visit led to Amy Piccoli's diagnosis with late-stage colorectal cancer.

entry.title: The Best Morning Drink for High Blood Pressure, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Discover the best morning drink for high blood pressure. Dietitians recommend beet juice for its heart health benefits, blood pressure support and key nutrients.

entry.title: 'This might be the point of no return': Experts on the current measles outbreak and where we go from here
entry.summary: Live Science spoke with two authors of a "progress report" detailing America's ongoing measles outbreak.

entry.title: Neuroscientists discover previously unknown cognitive benefits of reading physical books
entry.summary: A new study reveals that reading comic books on physical paper reduces the brain's workload compared to digital tablets. Brain scans show that paper provides sensory cues that make it easier to mentally organize and remember story details.

entry.title: The anti-aging peptide everyone is suddenly talking about
entry.summary: GHK-Cu is a copper peptide that supporters claim may help the body regenerate more effectively and reduce visible signs of aging. Here’s what to know.

entry.title: How much strength training do you really need to live longer? Not much
entry.summary: Health News: For years, the exercise advice most closely linked to living longer was simple: walk briskly, run if you can, cycle, swim, climb stairs, keep the hear.

entry.title: In Lancaster, measles cases are rising as providers try to combat low vaccination rates
entry.summary: Local doctors say they’re worried that even more cases are spreading under the radar in under-vaccinated pockets of Lancaster County, where 31 cases have been detected.

entry.title: Why the penis is a barometer of men's health
entry.summary: Erectile dysfunction can be an early warning of heart attack, stroke, diabetes and dementia.

entry.title: 5 Sneaky Signs You Might Need More Calcium, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Nutrition experts share the surprising signs that indicate you may be falling short on calcium alongside strategies to help boost your calcium intake.

entry.title: You'd Never Guess This Subtle Sign Of A Stroke
entry.summary: Stroke symptoms aren't always so obvious. Here's how to spot one coming on.

entry.title: Woman hears ringing in her ears, blames stress—then learns terrifying truth
entry.summary: Melony Aponte told Newsweek: “something felt off but I kept putting it down to stress."

entry.title: How AI is reshaping stem cell therapy through cellular intelligence
entry.summary: AI-native platforms are reshaping stem cell research, making regenerative medicine faster, cheaper, and more precise.

entry.title: A survey across 142 countries found that about almost a quarter of people felt very or fairly lonely, but the loneliest group was not the old, as many assume: it was young adults aged 19 to 29
entry.summary: Almost a quarter of the world reported feeling very or fairly lonely. The Meta-Gallup Global State of Social Connections survey (pulished in 2023) put the figure at 24% across 142 countries and areas. While this is shocking, perhaps the more interesting numbe…

entry.title: A Supplement For Joint Pain Could Accelerate Alzheimer's Memory Loss
entry.summary: People with Alzheimer's disease who took the common supplement glucosamine were 25% more likely to die within five years than those who didn't.

entry.title: Why tiny amounts of vitamin B12 matter more as we age
entry.summary: Two micrograms is an almost unimaginably small amount. It weighs less than a tiny fragment of a grain of table salt. Yet adults need only around this amount of vitamin B12 each day, depending on the guideline used, to support red blood cells, nerves and DNA p…

entry.title: First West Nile Virus-Positive Mosquitoes Reported In Chicago This Year
entry.summary: No humans have tested positive, per the Chicago Department of Public Health. The best way to prevent the illness is to prevent mosquito bites.

entry.title: What would it take to stop women from bleeding to death after childbirth?
entry.summary: A newly published series of reports calls attention to a dire situation facing millions of women after childbirth — and the solutions that can prevent death from postpartum hemorrhage.

entry.title: Fourth Person Infected by Emerging Tick-Bourne Disease
entry.summary: Another California resident tested positive for the bacteria Rickettsia lanei, as confirmed by The California Department of Public Health. This marks the...

entry.title: How the Unconscious Blocks Distressing Language
entry.summary: A new study reveals the unconscious mind suppresses negative spoken words to protect conscious focus.

entry.title: Californian Dies from Rare Disease After 'Trapping, Feeding and Breeding Wild Rats' in an RV
entry.summary: Another person fell ill in the incident, which the city manager said was an "extreme situation"

entry.title: Californian Dies from Rare Disease After 'Trapping, Feeding and Breeding Wild Rats' in an RV
entry.summary: Another person fell ill in the incident, which the city manager said was an "extreme situation"

entry.title: Scientist creates 'mini‑universe' to measure time without a clock
entry.summary: A University of Birmingham scientist has built a "mini-universe" that takes a step toward answering one of science's biggest questions: "What is time?" Publishing his findings in Physical Review Research, Professor Giovanni Barontini shows how it is possible …

entry.title: Bay Area still battling 'extremely contagious' norovirus
entry.summary: Traces of norovirus are at high levels in Palo Alto, San Jose, Fremont, Vallejo and Novato, June 11 wastewater data shows.
